summary:
📋 Weekly Review Digest

Issues Found
- crypto-trading — Daily runs halted; overseer-status.json stale ~6.4 days (no runs since 2026-07-07, failing silently). #34

Top Enhancement Ideas (ranked)
1. overseer — Per-project freshness SLA + staleness alerts in digest (effort: low, impact: high): the crypto feed sat 153h stale while overseer stayed quiet — surface silent staleness at top of digest. (Directly prevents repeats of issue #34.)
2. crypto-trading — Signal reject-funnel report from existing thresholds (effort: low, impact: high): 10 signals evaluated, 0 acted — show the binding constraint to tell mis-tuned from structurally idle.
3. ufc-dashboard — Detect fighter-swap/bout-replacement to suppress phantom alerts (effort: medium, impact: high): impossible magnitudes (983, -245) are substitutions diffed as odds moves — restore alert trust.
4. coachvision — Per-technique coaching feedback w/ confidence scores (effort: medium, impact: high): segmentation works but nothing consumes it — build the layer that gives users a reason to upload clips.
5. ufc-dashboard — Rank/badge alerts by main-event weight + time-to-event (effort: low, impact: medium): main events are buried under raw magnitude.
6. crypto-trading — Buy-and-hold benchmark + rolling alpha on equity curve (effort: low, impact: medium): scalars already exist (-10.7% alpha) — make underperformance visually obvious.

Trading bot.reason: read OK but no recent activity / stale data

coachvision.reason: read OK but no recent activity / stale data

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: Looking at the trading bot's status, it's marked as stale with an age of 153.7 hours — the last update was from July 7th while other systems are current as of July 13th. This indicates the daily scheduled runs have stopped working, which i…
timeline.text_full: Looking at the trading bot's status, it's marked as stale with an age of 153.7 hours — the last update was from July 7th while other systems are current as of July 13th. This indicates the daily scheduled runs have stopped working, which is a clear issue that needs investigation. For the volleyball system, it's just idle waiting for footage to be ingested, but the pipeline selftest passes, so there's no actual bug there. The UFC system is running healthily with a 1.0 success rate and recent activity, though there are some stale events that are just awaiting updates rather than indicating a failure. The overseer itself runs weekly and is slightly overdue based on the 167.6-hour gap since its last run, which aligns with the stale status flag I noted earlier. The trading bot is definitely stale at 153.7 hours old — that's 6.4 days without running when it should be on a daily schedule, so this is a real issue. Let me search the existing issues in the crypto-trading repo to see if this has already been reported.

timeline.label: search_existing_issues (search)
timeline.text: searched AndyRBrett/crypto-trading: 0 match(es)

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: No existing issues, so I should go ahead and file the bug.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: Looking at the crypto trading bot's performance, the strategy is lagging behind a simple buy-and-hold approach by about 10.7%, and while it's generating signals, it's not actually executing on them. I'm considering a few approaches to fix …
timeline.text_full: Looking at the crypto trading bot's performance, the strategy is lagging behind a simple buy-and-hold approach by about 10.7%, and while it's generating signals, it's not actually executing on them. I'm considering a few approaches to fix this: implementing a walk-forward backtest framework to optimize parameters, adding a regime filter that switches to buy-and-hold when alpha turns negative, or investigating why the signal execution rate is zero. The most practical starting point seems to be an alpha-based throttle that automatically adjusts trading activity based on performance. For immediate visibility, I could add a visualization showing why signals were rejected and how close they came to triggering—this would help diagnose the execution gap without major refactoring. On the coachvision side, the pipeline works fine in testing but no actual footage has been ingested yet. The quickest wins would be setting up folder monitoring with notifications when the system sits idle, or adding a sample onboarding clip to guide users through the upload process. A more complete enhancement would generate per-technique confidence scores and coaching feedback summaries once footage starts flowing in. The UFC dashboard is performing well overall, but the line movement alerts are picking up a lot of noise—some movements of 983 points are clearly data artifacts from fighter swaps rather than genuine market shifts. I should add a sanity filter to detect bout replacements and focus alerts on main-event movements that actually matter. For Overseer, the real issue is staleness. It only runs weekly and the crypto feed is over 150 hours old, which defeats the purpose of monitoring. I could add freshness SLA checks per project with staleness alerting, or set up a weekly digest that surfaces stale feeds via email or Slack. Either approach would catch these gaps without much effort. Now I'm thinking through which improvements to prioritize based on impact versus effort. I've got the bench…

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: I'm reviewing the findings from the bug hunter and noting that the staleness issue in crypto-trading #34 relates to the overseer freshness SLA idea, though they're distinct — one's the bug itself, the other's an enhancement to detect it. I…
timeline.text_full: I'm reviewing the findings from the bug hunter and noting that the staleness issue in crypto-trading #34 relates to the overseer freshness SLA idea, though they're distinct — one's the bug itself, the other's an enhancement to detect it. I'll keep both and document their connection, then compile the digest with the identified issue showing the daily runs have stalled with status data from over six days ago. Now I'm ranking the ideas by effort and impact, leading with the overseer freshness SLA since it's directly motivated by this week's crypto bug. I'm considering what to keep — seven ideas is too many for a focused digest, so I'm trimming down to the strongest six, dropping the overseer unit tests since it's medium effort and medium impact, even though it relates to the staleness problem.

timeline.text: The tool came back with a not_configured status after my first call, so the delivery failed due to missing configuration. Since I'm limited to one attempt, I'll report this result.
